name: ಡಿ.ಎಸ್. ಕರ್ಕಿ
bio: D.S. Karki (1907-1984) was a popular Kannada poet known for his patriotic and rhythmic songs. His most famous work is 'Hacchevu Kannadada Deepa'.

name: ಕೆ.ಎಸ್. ನಿಸಾರ್ ಅಹಮದ್
bio: K.S. Nissar Ahmed (1936-2020) was a prominent Kannada poet and writer, awarded Padma Shri in 2008. He is famous for his work 'Nityotsava'.

name: ಕುವೆಂಪು
bio: K.V. Puttappa (1904-1994), pen name Kuvempu, was the first Kannada writer to receive the Jnanpith Award and is honored as Rashtrakavi.

name: ದ.ರಾ. ಬೇಂದ್ರೆ
bio: D.R. Bendre (1896-1981), known as Ambikatanayadatta, was a legendary poet of the Navodaya movement and a Jnanpith Award winner.

name: ಬಸವಣ್ಣ
bio: Basavanna (12th century) was a philosopher, social reformer, and pioneer of the Bhakti movement. He introduced Vachana literature.

name: ಡಿ.ವಿ. ಗುಂಡಪ್ಪ
bio: D.V. Gundappa (1887-1975), known as DVG, was a great scholar and philosopher, best known for the philosophical collection 'Mankuthimmana Kagga'.

name: ಕೆ.ಎಸ್. ನರಸಿಂಹಸ್ವಾಮಿ
bio: K.S. Narasimhaswamy (1915-2003) was a popular poet known for his romantic and simple lyrics, especially the 'Mysuru Mallige' collection.

name: ಜಿ.ಎಸ್. ಶಿವರುದ್ರಪ್ಪ
bio: G.S. Shivarudrappa (1926-2013) was a poet, researcher, and recipient of the Rashtrakavi title, known for his balanced and modern poetic style.

name: ಅಕ್ಕಮಹಾದೇವಿ
bio: Akka Mahadevi (12th century) was a prominent poet and mystic of the Veerashaiva movement, known for her intense spiritual Vachanas.

name: ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ
bio: Sarvajna (16th century) was a wandering poet and philosopher famous for his three-line poems (Tripadis) that offer practical wisdom.

name: ಕನಕದಾಸರು
bio: Kanakadasa (16th century) was a poet, philosopher, and saint of the Haridasa movement, known for his social reformist Keerthanas.

name: ಪುರಂದರದಾಸರು
bio: Purandara Dasa (1484-1564) is hailed as the 'Pitamaha' of Carnatic Music and was a prolific Haridasa saint.

name: ಪಿ. ಲಂಕೇಶ್
bio: P. Lankesh (1935-2000) was an editor, writer, and poet known for his sharp social criticism and powerful modern poetry like 'Avva'.

name: ಅಲ್ಲಮಪ್ರಭು
bio: Allama Prabhu was a 12th-century mystic and Vachana poet, serving as the president of Anubhava Mantapa.

name: ಮಾಸ್ತಿ ವೆಂಕಟೇಶ ಅಯ್ಯಂಗಾರ್
bio: Masti Venkatesha Iyengar (1891-1986), a Jnanpith winner, is considered the 'Grandfather of Kannada Short Stories'.

name: ಜಿ.ಪಿ. ರಾಜರತ್ನಂ
bio: G.P. Rajarathnam (1909-1979) was a poet who used colloquial language to reach the common man, famous for 'Ratnana Padagalu'.

name: ಸಿದ್ಧರಾಮ
bio: Siddharama (12th century) was a prominent Vachana poet and social worker from Solapur, dedicated to the service of mankind.
